text:
There once was a young girl named Lily. Whenever someone sneezed, she'd say "Bless you!" in a cheerful voice. One day, while playing outside, Lily noticed something unusual. There were tiny birds fluttering just indoors.

She watched them flitter around an old rocking chair, and one chirped loudly enough to startle her. Curious, she approached. The bird gave her a tiny feather, saying something indiscernible but so precious. Lily didn't want to lose it, so she promised to let it fly free again.

On her next visit, the bird was gone. She searched everywhere. But when she told her mother, she got just the kind of laugh you'd expect. "Oh, my dear," her mother chuckled. "You just caught yourself a mousy little star."

Lily didn't believe that for a moment. Next day she set out with a net. She caught a few. Her collection grew fast. She'd call each one
